get.description:
Returns the current version and git information
This endpoint does not require authentication

post.description:
Authenticates a user with username and password

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 29.2, 30.4
Invalidates the user's session and clears the session cookie

get.description: Returns authenticated user information or 401 if not authenticated

Exchanges the authorization code for tokens and creates a session

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 29.2, 30.4

Redirects the user to the OIDC provider's authorization endpoint

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 29.2

get.summary: Get shards information using SDK typed methods
get.description:
Returns shards info in frontend-compatible format

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 4.8

get.summary: Get shards allocated on a specific node
get.description:
Returns shard information for shards on the specified node only.
This is more efficient than fetching all shards and filtering client-side.

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 4.8

get.summary: Get detailed stats for a specific node
get.description:
Returns comprehensive node statistics including thread pools, shards, and metrics

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 14.3, 14.4, 14.5

Forwards the request to the specified cluster and returns the response

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 2.16, 29.3

post.summary: Relocate a shard from one node to another
post.description:
Executes the Elasticsearch cluster reroute API to move a shard

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 6.1, 6.2, 6.3, 6.4, 6.5, 6.6, 6.7, 6.8, 6.9, 6.10, 6.11, 8.1, 8.2, 8.3, 8.4

get.description:
Returns cluster settings in JSON format
Optionally includes default settings with `include_defaults` query parameter

put.description:
Updates persistent and/or transient cluster settings
Only modified settings need to be provided in the request body

get.summary: Get cluster statistics using SDK typed methods
get.description:
Returns cluster stats in frontend-compatible format

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 4.1, 4.2, 4.3

get.summary: List all active tasks in a cluster
get.description:
# Requirements

Validates: Requirement 1, 2, 3 (Task display with filtering)

parameters.name: type_filter
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of task types to filter

parameters.name: action_filter
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of task actions to filter

get.summary: Get detailed information about a specific task

get.description:
Returns HTTP 200 if the server is healthy
This endpoint does not require authentication

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 39.1, 39.2, 39.5

post.description:
Tests connection to a Prometheus endpoint and validates it can be reached.
Used to verify configuration before saving.

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 2.0, 2.1

get.description:
Returns metrics for a specific cluster.
Currently uses internal Elasticsearch metrics.
Future: Will support Prometheus metrics based on cluster configuration.

# Query Parameters
- `start` - Unix timestamp (seconds) for range start. Defaults to 24 hours ago.
- `end` - Unix timestamp (seconds) for range end. Defaults to current time.

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 1.0, 1.1

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start timestamp (Unix seconds). If not provided, defaults to 24 hours ago

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End timestamp (Unix seconds). If not provided, defaults to now

get.summary: Get cluster metrics history (for heatmap visualization)
get.description:
Returns cluster health status snapshots over a time period for heatmap visualization.
Currently returns placeholder data. Will be populated with Prometheus historical data.

# Query Parameters
- `start` - Unix timestamp (seconds) for range start. Defaults to 7 days ago.
- `end` - Unix timestamp (seconds) for range end. Defaults to current time.

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 3.0

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start timestamp (Unix seconds). If not provided, defaults to 24 hours ago

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End timestamp (Unix seconds). If not provided, defaults to now

get.description:
Returns time series metrics for a specific node when cluster uses Prometheus metrics source.

# Query Parameters
- `start` - Unix timestamp (seconds) for range start. Defaults to 24 hours ago.
- `end` - Unix timestamp (seconds) for range end. Defaults to current time.

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 1.0, 1.1

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start timestamp (Unix seconds). If not provided, defaults to 24 hours ago

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End timestamp (Unix seconds). If not provided, defaults to now

get.description:
Returns HTTP 200 if the server is ready to accept requests
This endpoint does not require authentication

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 39.3, 39.6

healthy.description: Number of clusters that are healthy

unhealthy.description: Number of clusters that are unhealthy

prometheus_queries.description: Prometheus queries used for each metric (metric_name -> full query)

oneOf.description: Raw metric points with labels for multi-series metrics (e.g., memory by area)

ClusterMetricsPoint.description: Cluster metrics data point for frontend consumption

ClusterSettingsUpdateRequest.description: Request body for updating cluster settings

transient.description: Transient settings (do not survive cluster restarts)

ClustersQueryParams.description:
List all configured clusters

Returns a list of all clusters with filtering and pagination

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 2.15

IndicesQueryParams.description:
Get indices information using SDK typed methods with pagination

Returns paginated indices info in frontend-compatible format

Validates: Requirements 4.7

LabeledMetricPoint.description: Metric point with optional labels for multi-series support

end.description: End timestamp (Unix seconds). If not provided, defaults to now

start.description: Start timestamp (Unix seconds). If not provided, defaults to 24 hours ago

NodeDetailStatsResponse.description: Node detail stats response for frontend

prometheus_queries.description: Prometheus queries used for each metric (metric_name -> full query)

NodeMetricsPoint.description: Node metrics data point for frontend consumption

Get nodes information using SDK typed methods with pagination

Returns paginated nodes info in frontend-compatible format

Validates: Requirements 4.6, 14.1, 14.2

total.description: Total count of all items across all pages
Total count of all items across all pages
Total count of all items across all pages

items.description:
Shard info response for frontend

Validates: Requirements 9.1, 9.2, 9.3

docs.description: Document count - always present, 0 if unavailable (Requirement 9.3)

store.description: Store size in bytes - always present, 0 if unavailable (Requirement 9.3)

total.description: Total count of all items across all pages

job_name.description: Optional job name for metric filtering

RawMetrics.description: Raw metric data with labels for flexible frontend rendering

cpu.description: CPU usage points with labels (e.g., node=node-1, node=node-2)

memory.description: Memory usage points with labels (e.g., area=heap, area=non-heap)

message.example: Server is ready to accept requests

ShardsQueryParams.description:
Get shards information for a cluster with pagination and filtering

# Requirements

Validates: Requirements 4.8

raw.description: Raw Elasticsearch response for full details

TaskDetails.description:
Detailed task information from Elasticsearch Tasks Get API

Includes full task details and raw JSON response

TaskDetailsResponse.description: Response from get task details endpoint

message.example: Failed to get cluster: Connection refused

TaskInfo.description:
Task information from Elasticsearch Tasks API

Represents a single active task in the cluster

cancellable.description: Whether the task can be cancelled

cancelled.description: Whether the task is already cancelled

id.description: Unique task ID within the node

node.description: Node ID where the task is running

parent_task_id.description: Parent task ID if this is a subtask

running_time_millis.description: Running time in milliseconds (calculated client-side if needed)

start_time_in_millis.description: Start time in milliseconds since epoch

action_filter.description: Comma-separated list of task actions to filter

type_filter.description: Comma-separated list of task types to filter
